summary: In Couinaud’s liver segmentation system, while vascular structures like the portal vein contribute significantly, the bile duct primarily defines the anatomical distinctions among segments by delineating areas drained specifically by individual branches. \boxed{\text{Bile duct}} This summary captures the essence of why the bile duct stands out as essential for understanding segment differentiation despite other vital roles played by vessels such as the portal vein or hepatic arteries.
